label: fiction
title: The Borrowed Voice
setting: A workplace extortion trial in 2026, testing whether a synthetic recording was fabricated by outsiders or assembled on the accused’s own laptop.
charge: Using synthetic audio to extort money from a colleague
elements: The accused performed or participated in the alleged act.
The act was deliberate or dishonest rather than accidental.
The prosecution has proved every required fact beyond reasonable doubt.
hook: The voice on the call belongs to a manager. The fear in it belongs to a colleague. The breaths belong to someone else.

accused.human: Evan Rusk records local theatre and has spent years teaching free sound workshops to young performers.
accused.if_guilty: A conviction means prison, loss of a creative career, and permanent suspicion around every recording he has made.

prosecution.speaker: pc
prosecution.text: The prosecution says the accused built a cloned voice, inserted his own whispered prompts, and demanded payment using private workplace details. The apparent centre of the case is a polished synthetic recording resembling the manager, but the evidence must be followed through the complete sequence rather than treated as a single dramatic clue.

defence.speaker: dc
defence.text: The defence says an audio worker was framed with common tools and copied samples after criticising weak security around shared production files. Suspicion gathered around a person before investigators tested the limits of a polished synthetic recording resembling the manager; those limits matter because a criminal verdict cannot rest on an attractive shortcut.

prosecution.speaker: pc
prosecution.text: The separate records now meet at the same point. the source breaths captured on the accused’s own microphone does not depend on rumour or hindsight, and it fits the conduct before and after the event. Taken together, the prosecution says the elements are proved beyond reasonable doubt.

defence.speaker: dc
defence.text: The prosecution still asks you to bridge uncertainty with suspicion. a polished synthetic recording resembling the manager looked powerful at first, yet every witness accepted limits around it. If the remaining record supports a reasonable innocent account, your duty is to return not guilty.

epilogue: The colleague received counselling and returned to work on a different team. Evan’s workshop continued under new leadership, while the studio adopted consent records and access logs for every voice model built from a performer. The people involved kept living with the consequences after the courtroom emptied. The case changed workplace rules, but it could not return the months spent under accusation.

beats.speaker: w1
beats.text: The caller used my manager’s voice and quoted a private mistake from my review. It demanded money, then played a recording suggesting my job would disappear if I reported it. I recorded that detail before anyone suggested what my evidence should mean. I remember it because it was unusual, and I have not changed my account.
beats.reveal_note: The fake tracking numbers were the tell the room kept circling back to: a supplier failing you does not require you to post decoy socks.

beats.speaker: w2
beats.text: The voice model was built from public interviews. That source material was widely available, so the synthetic voice itself does not identify who assembled the call. I recorded that detail before anyone suggested what my evidence should mean. I remember it because it was unusual, and I have not changed my account. That is what I personally saw, heard, or did; I cannot take it further.
beats.reveal_note: One buyer's story, multiplied by two hundred and sixty-nine. The pattern was the crime; she was one data point of it who could cry.

beats.speaker: dc
beats.text: I isolated four breaths and a whispered count beneath the generated speech. Their microphone profile and room reflection match a tutorial recorded on Evan’s laptop that evening. The record was created at the time and its origin is not disputed. The jury saw the complete item, including the limits written beside the result. Nothing in that account depends on a later reconstruction of events.
beats.reveal_note: A convincing paper trail of victimhood — until the device forensics showed both sides of the conversation being typed on the same keyboard.

beats.speaker: w1
beats.text: A cloned voice can be built from public material? Yes. The manager’s public interviews were enough. So the voice does not identify Evan? Not by itself; the underlying source layers are the stronger evidence. I gave that answer in my first statement and it remains my evidence today. I gave that answer in my first statement and it remains my evidence today.

turns.speaker: dc
turns.text: A cloned voice can be built from public material?

turns.speaker: w1
turns.text: Yes. The manager’s public interviews were enough.

turns.speaker: dc
turns.text: So the voice does not identify Evan?

turns.speaker: w1
turns.text: Not by itself; the underlying source layers are the stronger evidence. I gave that answer in my first statement and it remains my evidence today. I gave that answer in my first statement and it remains my evidence today.

beats.reveal_note: The early honest shipments were real — and standard. Exit scams buy their reviews first; the clean history was the runway, not the rebuttal.

beats.speaker: pc
beats.text: The project folder was deleted, but temporary render files preserve the order of layers: cloned voice, Evan’s breath track, then a tone masking the edit points. The record was created at the time and its origin is not disputed. The jury saw the complete item, including the limits written beside the result. Nothing in that account depends on a later reconstruction of events.
beats.reveal_note: He was corresponding with himself and rehearsing the supplier's lines in advance. The middleman and the scammer shared a notes app.

beats.speaker: pc
beats.text: I recorded tutorials for dozens of people and shared equipment. A matching microphone profile proves a room and device, not that I made this threatening call. The record was created at the time and its origin is not disputed. The jury saw the complete item, including the limits written beside the result. Nothing in that account depends on a later reconstruction of events.
beats.reveal_note: A middleman's money flows through him to a supplier. This money flowed to a car lease. There was never anything upstream.

beats.speaker: w3
beats.text: The laptop security log shows Evan’s hardware key unlocking the session. The key was used again to export the final audio minutes before the extortion message. I recorded that detail before anyone suggested what my evidence should mean. I remember it because it was unusual, and I have not changed my account. That is what I personally saw, heard, or did; I cannot take it further.
beats.reveal_note: 'Buying time' was the confession wearing a coat: time was the one thing every exit scam needs, and the decoy parcels bought exactly four more weeks of orders.

beats.speaker: w3
beats.text: Your microphone was used for many tutorials? Yes, by students and contractors. Your hardware key was sometimes left in the console? During supervised sessions, but not overnight when this export occurred. I gave that answer in my first statement and it remains my evidence today. I gave that answer in my first statement and it remains my evidence today.

turns.speaker: pc
turns.text: Your microphone was used for many tutorials?

turns.speaker: pc
turns.text: Your hardware key was sometimes left in the console?

turns.speaker: w3
turns.text: During supervised sessions, but not overnight when this export occurred. I gave that answer in my first statement and it remains my evidence today. I gave that answer in my first statement and it remains my evidence today.

beats.reveal_note: The third invisible stranger of the week: someone who used his details, on his phone, in his notes app, days ahead of schedule.

beats.speaker: w4
beats.text: Our studio left sample drives in open bays. Contractors could copy voices, room tones, and old tutorials without creating a central access record. I recorded that detail before anyone suggested what my evidence should mean. I remember it because it was unusual, and I have not changed my account. That is what I personally saw, heard, or did; I cannot take it further.
beats.reveal_note: Her grief was real and her evidence was secondhand: the distress she saw fits a scammer watching his exit close just as well as a victim watching his business die.

beats.speaker: judge
beats.text: The prosecution must prove every element beyond reasonable doubt. The accused does not have to prove an alternative account, explain every suspicious detail, or show who else was responsible. Treat digital records like any other evidence: examine their origin, limits, and connection to a person. The record was created at the time and its origin is not disputed.
beats.reveal_note: The direction gave his defence its full width — a real middleman scam would have acquitted him. The notes app took that defence away.

twist: The cloned voice could have been made by many people with access to public recordings. Beneath it were four breaths and a whispered count captured by Evan’s microphone, carrying the same room resonance as a tutorial he recorded alone that evening.

jurors.label: Juror 2
